title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues · History Brief: Gita Stacked Change Dependencies
summary: A distributed project receives dependent reviews, rerolls, hotfixes, subsystem branches, and release candidates from contributors with different trust and access. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita stacked change dependencies increment in stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Stacked Change Dependencies
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G23 is a theory in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Stacked Change Dependencies contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G23 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published. A defensible solution applies Gita Stacked Change Dependencies, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 3
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies
git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2
# expected: old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible
# observation: one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent
# changed-case: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published
# transfer-limit: provider merge queues need separate event, identity, permission, and tested-revision verification
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A stack is independently reviewable whenever its final tip builds, even if intermediate commits do not. This fails the Gita Stacked Change Dependencies contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Gita Stacked Change Dependencies evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-stacked-change-dependencies-fklmrj" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Stacked Change Dependencies into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-stacked-change-dependencies-fklmrj
# scenario-key: trace-fklmrj probe-jrmlkf boundary-lmrjfk owner-fklmrjx fault-fklmrjz
# before-ref: main=a11cefk candidate=b00b1rj
# hypothesis: the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies
git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2
# expected: old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible
# observation: one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent
# changed-case: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree
# after-ref: main=a11cefk candidate=c0ffeej
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published
# transfer-limit: provider merge queues need separate event, identity, permission, and tested-revision verification
